steps.title: Map the structure
steps.content: The first sentence gives a broad trend: high-speed rail is expanding in many countries because it can reduce automobile use and conserve energy. The second sentence narrows to Morocco, and the phrase for instance signals that Morocco is an example of the broader trend.

steps.title: Check the structure claims
steps.content: Choice B is wrong because Morocco supports the opening claim instead of challenging an argument. Choice C is wrong because the text does not question high-speed rail or suggest alternatives. Choice D is wrong because no long-held belief is rejected. The answer is A.
